title_english: The Tale of Two Bad Mice

characters.name: Tom Thumb
characters.description: A small brown mouse, Hunca Munca's husband. Bold and quick-tempered. Often shown upright on hind legs.
characters.source: Tom Thumb from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Two Bad Mice (1904)

characters.name: Hunca Munca
characters.description: A small brown mouse, Tom Thumb's wife. Practical and frugal-minded. Wears a patterned dress and apron in later scenes.
characters.source: Hunca Munca from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Two Bad Mice (1904)

characters.name: Lucinda
characters.description: A tall wooden doll with dark hair, wearing a pink floral dress. Owner of the dollhouse. Stiff and proper.
characters.source: Lucinda from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Two Bad Mice

characters.name: Jane
characters.description: A smaller wooden doll with blonde hair and a blue bow, wearing a blue dress. The cook doll. Always smiling.
characters.source: Jane from Beatrix Potter's The Tale of Two Bad Mice

locations.name: The Dollhouse
locations.description: A beautiful red brick dollhouse with white windows, real muslin curtains, a front door, and a chimney. Contains a dining room with laid table, a kitchen, and bedrooms upstairs.

locations.name: The Nursery
locations.description: The room where the dollhouse stands, by a fireplace. The mouse-hole is under the skirting-board near the fireplace.

locations.name: The Mouse-Hole
locations.description: Tom Thumb and Hunca Munca's home, behind the skirting-board near the fireplace.

pages.english_text: This is a beautiful dollhouse. It has red walls and white windows.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_004.jpg] The red brick dollhouse with white windows and muslin curtains. Two dolls visible outside — Lucinda standing on the roof balcony, Jane sitting below.

pages.english_text: Two dolls lived there: Lucinda and Jane. Jane was the cook.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_005.jpg] The two dolls — tall Lucinda in pink and smaller Jane in blue — standing by their packing case. Proper and stiff.

pages.english_text: There was pretty food on the table: a ham, a fish, and a pudding.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_006.jpg] Close-up of the dollhouse dining table laden with beautiful toy food — ham, lobsters, fish, pudding, pears, oranges on tiny plates.

pages.english_text: One morning the dolls went out. The house was quiet.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_007.jpg] Tom Thumb popping his head out of the mouse-hole near the fireplace, peering cautiously into the quiet nursery.

pages.english_text: Tom Thumb was a mouse. Hunca Munca was his wife.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_008.jpg] Both mice peering out of their hole together, looking across the nursery toward the dollhouse. Alert, curious, planning.

pages.english_text: The mice crept up to the dollhouse. They pushed the door open!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_009.jpg] The two mice creeping cautiously across the hearthrug toward the red dollhouse, approaching the front door.

pages.english_text: They found the dining room. What a feast!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_010.jpg] Tom Thumb and Hunca Munca peering into the dollhouse dining room, seeing the table set with beautiful food. Their eyes wide with delight.

pages.english_text: Tom Thumb tried to cut the ham. The knife bent!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_011.jpg] Tom Thumb sitting at the table, pressing a lead knife into the shiny painted ham. Hunca Munca watches from behind. The knife crumples.

pages.english_text: Hunca Munca tried too. The ham was very hard!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_012.jpg] Hunca Munca standing on a chair, chopping at the ham with a lead knife. Tom Thumb watches. Both frustrated.

pages.english_text: The ham fell off the plate and rolled under the table.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_013.jpg] The ham broken off the plate, rolling under the table. Tom Thumb leans down from his chair to look. Hunca Munca annoyed.

pages.english_text: Tom Thumb was angry! He smashed the food with a shovel. It was all plaster!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_014.jpg] Tom Thumb in a rage, hitting the plates with tongs and a shovel. Broken plaster food everywhere. Hunca Munca putting the fish into the fire. Chaos and destruction.

pages.english_text: They put the fish in the fire. It would not burn!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_015.jpg] Hunca Munca pushing the fish into the red crinkly paper fire in the dollhouse kitchen. The fish will not burn because it is painted tin.

pages.english_text: Tom Thumb went up the chimney. There was no soot!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_016.jpg] Tom Thumb looking out from the top of the dollhouse chimney, bewildered. Nothing is real in this house.

pages.english_text: Hunca Munca found little cans. But they were empty inside!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_017.jpg] Hunca Munca turning canisters upside down on the dresser. Labeled Rice, Coffee, Sago — but only beads fall out. Another disappointment.

pages.english_text: The mice were very angry. Tom Thumb threw everything out the window!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_018.jpg] Tom Thumb throwing clothes and objects out the upstairs window of the dollhouse. Wild mischief and destruction.

pages.english_text: Hunca Munca took the feather bed home to the mouse-hole.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_019.jpg] The two mice carrying the large bolster/feather bed down the dollhouse stairs. Struggling to squeeze it through. Hunca Munca's practical side.

pages.english_text: She took a cradle and a birdcage too!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_021.jpg] Hunca Munca carrying the cradle away. The birdcage left behind the coal-box because it would not fit through the mouse-hole.

pages.english_text: Then the dolls came home. What a mess!
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_023.jpg] Lucinda and Jane standing in their wrecked dollhouse — Lucinda staring, Jane smiling blankly. Broken food, missing furniture, chaos everywhere.

pages.english_text: But the mice were sorry. Tom Thumb found a coin and paid for everything he broke.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_028.jpg] Tom Thumb and Hunca Munca together, stuffing a crooked sixpence into a tiny stocking. The dolls visible in bed behind them. A quiet act of restitution.

pages.english_text: And every morning, Hunca Munca sweeps the dollhouse clean.
pages.scene_description: [IMAGE: image_029.jpg] Hunca Munca in her dress and apron, standing in the dollhouse doorway with a broom and dustpan. Faithful restitution, every single morning.
